symbols: altar — the place of sacrifice and the Eucharistic table where heaven and earth meet
gilded altarpiece — the glory of God made visible through sacred art, instructing the faithful
stone walls — the enduring house of God, the living stones of the Church (1 Peter 2:5)
narrow windows admitting light — divine illumination penetrating the darkness of the fallen world
apse vault — the dome of heaven sheltering the sanctuary of God's presence
iron railing — the boundary between the nave (earthly realm) and the sanctuary (holy of holies)
description: A dim interior of a Romanesque stone church viewed from the nave toward the apse. Thick stone walls rise to a rounded barrel vault. Two narrow slit windows on the left admit faint natural light. At the far end, a gilded Gothic altarpiece with multiple panels and carved figures sits upon the altar, illuminated softly. A low iron railing separates the sanctuary from the nave. Red-cushioned wooden pews or benches line either side. The floor appears to be stone. The overall atmosphere is one of deep antiquity, silence, and austere devotion.

scripture_refs.reason: 'How lovely is your dwelling place, O Lord of hosts! My soul longs, yes, faints for the courts of the Lord' -- the universal longing evoked by sacred architecture.
'You yourselves like living stones are being built up as a spiritual house' -- the stone church as visible sign of the invisible Church.
The glory of the Lord filling the temple of Solomon -- every consecrated church participates in this indwelling presence.

_meta.title: Interior of Nes Church, Ringsaker, Norway
